endpoint_validation.description: Validate backend API endpoints for common issues and security vulnerabilities

best_practices.description: Check backend code against industry best practices
rules: Proper HTTP method usage (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
best_practices.prompt_template:
Review the following backend code against industry best practices. Identify violations and suggest improvements:

File: {{file_path}}
Code:
{{code_content}}

Check against these best practices:
{{rules_list}}

Provide:
1. Issues found
2. Severity (critical, high, medium, low)
3. Recommended fixes with code examples
4. Root cause analysis

metrics: Proper use of constants and enums
code_quality.prompt_template:
Evaluate the code quality of the following file:

File: {{file_path}}
Code:
{{code_content}}

Assess these quality metrics:
{{metrics_list}}

Provide:
1. Quality score (0-100)
2. Critical issues
3. Refactoring recommendations
4. Code smell detection

component_validation.description: Validate frontend components for best practices

ui_best_practices.description: Check frontend code against UI/UX best practices
rules: User feedback mechanisms (loading spinners, success/error messages)
Proper form field labels and validation messages
SEO meta tags and semantic HTML

security_audit.description: Comprehensive security audit across the codebase
security_audit.prompt_template:
Perform a security audit on this code:

File: {{file_path}}
Code:
{{code_content}}

Check for these vulnerabilities:
{{vulnerabilities_list}}

Provide:
1. Vulnerability description
2. Severity (CVSS score if applicable)
3. Proof of concept
4. Remediation steps with code

performance_analysis.description: Identify performance bottlenecks and optimization opportunities
areas: Database query optimization (N+1 queries, missing indexes)
performance_analysis.prompt_template:
Analyze performance of this code:

File: {{file_path}}
Code:
{{code_content}}

Identify issues in:
{{areas_list}}

Provide:
1. Bottleneck description
2. Impact assessment
3. Optimization approach
4. Expected improvement

severity_levels.critical: Immediate attention required - security vulnerability or system-breaking issue
severity_levels.high: Should be fixed soon - significant impact on functionality or security
severity_levels.medium: Should be addressed - moderate impact on code quality or performance
severity_levels.low: Nice to have - minor improvements or style issues
